rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (596-598 Haight Street, San Francisco, Ca 94117)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. According to the supplement, the sale was made in November 2024, but the entity purchaser identified in the petition was not in legal existence until 04/02/2025 (when the LLC formation document was filed in Delaware). It is unclear who signed the contract to purchase this property in November 2024, and whether the original purchaser has assigned all contractual rights to the LLC, as no assignment of the contract signed by the original purchaser in favor of the LLC is on file. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM JAMES ENGLAND
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/6/25. Examiner's Notes sent to counsel with the following issues: 1) Please confirm that all assets listed in the Inventory and Appraisal filed on 1/6/25 are cash assets (which they do not appear to be). If they are cash assets, an explanation will need to be provided for why the assets were described as non-cash assets in the I&A. If they are not all cash assets, the non-cash assets will need to be appraised by the probate referee. 2) All trust beneficiaries must be listed, with their addresses, and notice given to them. Sufficiency of notice filed on 2/19/25 will be reviewed once this issue is addressed. 3) Petitioner's request for costs in the amount of $2,634.55 must be itemized. 4) The cash to be distributed must be specified and the calculation provided (starting from $561,754.48 cash alleged to be on hand as of 2/10/25). 5) Proposed order has not been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSA ELVIRA SELPH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/20/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete legal description, street address (if any), and assessor's parcel number (if any) of real property. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (2) Attorney costs are not shown in four columns showing the date, payee, description, and amount. E-filing fees without a description of what was e-filed are not helpful. The executor presumably has approved the costs, since he verified the petition, but attorney costs that the Court is to approve must be set forth in detail. (3) The waiver of accounting was signed by the executor, not the trustee of the devisee trust. (4) Paragraph 34(d) of the petition is incomplete, as there is more than just cash to be distributed. (Paragraph 34 does not make sense, as Rosa Elvira is the decedent.) Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/6/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (When an accounting is waived, there is no need to file bank statements.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE HELEN L. WASHINGTON REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Trustee Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The judicial officer is to determine whether to take this petition off calendar for lack of progress, as nothing has been filed to address the issues (see tentative ruling for 01/08/2025 hearing): 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form (notice of hearing with proof of service) is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing. 3) Petitioner's verification must be signed with language required by CCP section 2015.5 (state the place of execution within California or refer to "under the laws of the State of California"). 4) Please provide a copy of letters issued to the petitioner as guardian of the estate of the minor beneficiary identified in the petition. If there is no guardianship of the estate, an ex parte petition must be filed to appoint an independent attorney as guardian ad litem to represent the minor's interest and join in this petition. See LRSF 14.11(F) & 14.14(C) for procedural requirements applicable to ex parte petititions for appointment of a guardian ad litem. 5) The petition must be amended to identify the minor beneficiary as an additional petitioner and necesssary party with standing, regardless of whether the guardian of the estate or guardian ad litem acts as representative of the minor beneficiary as a necessary party. 6) Is the petitioner the only person having legal custody of and residing with the minor beneficiary? If any other person has joint legal custody of and resides with the minor beneficiary (e.g., other parent), that person must be identified by a verified allegation as a person entitled to a separate copy of notice. See Probate Code section 17201 & CRC 7.902; see also CRC 7.51(d). 7) Please provide the settlor's date of death. 8) Please provide the names and addresses of the settlor's legal heirs, together with their relationship to the settlor. 9) The name and address of each living child of the settlor and living issue of the settlor's predeceassed child must be identiifed by a verified allegation as a beneficiary under Article 5C of the trust, and notice must be given to each beneficiary. 10) Each attachment to the petition, including a copy of the trust, must be identified as what it purports to be and verified as such. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AHMED NAGI ALI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/14/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Items 1, 2, and 2b of the petition are incomplete and must be addressed in a supplement, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner. (2) Item 2 of the Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is incomplete. (3) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/30/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] Since the petitioner is reportedly the sole heir and, therefore, not required to give himself notice, this defect will not need to be addressed. Please make a note of this local rule for future, should another party be entitled to notice.
